title: Investigate integrity→decider handoff failure. Integrity wrote 5 findings to its 2026-05-15T01:16Z report; decider ran at 01:30Z (14 minutes later) with fresh access but created ZERO ISSs for any of the findings. Same pattern likely caused yesterday's 4 findings to also go un-ISSed. Step 1d wiring from PROP-022/task #91 may have regressed, OR decider-on-Sonnet may have a model-class compatibility issue with the intake path.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews PROP before implementation. Tinker investigates the root cause, confirms which hypothesis is correct (regression vs model-class), proposes the canonical fix as PROP-NNN, does NOT self-apply. Decider's intake flow is load-bearing — getting it wrong on the design side has cascade effects, so operator wants tinker's analysis before any edits land.
context: Empirical observation 2026-05-15T05:00Z: integrity 01:16Z report flagged 5 findings (1 CRITICAL build drift, 2 MAJOR — tracker allocator collision + 4 orphan EXPs, 1 MODERATE — EXP-349 stale, 1 MINOR — workspace artifacts). Every finding's report ends with 'tracked_under: null → decider should create ISS'. The handoff is documented in decider-intake.md Step 1d (added in May 2026 per task #91 'Integrity moderate-findings handoff gap').

Decider ran 01:30Z, 14 minutes AFTER integrity wrote its report. Decider had fresh access. Its 01:30 daily-report.issues_created list contains ZERO ISSs from integrity findings. Specifically: the URGENT 'next_id=384 collision imminent' MAJOR finding was sitting in plain text in integrity/latest-integrity-summary.txt at 01:16Z; decider could have prevented a silent overwrite of EXP-384 with a one-line ISS + patch. Did neither.

Operator manually applied all 5 findings at 05:00-05:45Z this morning (commits c7b1274 + ecdbcb2): patched next_id 384→391, backfilled 4 orphan tracker entries, flipped EXP-349 status, ran build.js publish for the drift. That was operator labor that the integrity→decider handoff is supposed to make unnecessary.

Yesterday (2026-05-14) integrity also reported findings — the build drift, the tracker allocator (less urgent then, next_id=365 vs max=369), the workspace artifacts, the orphan EXPs. Decider's 2026-05-14T01:27 + 21:29 reports also created zero ISSs for these. So this is at least a 24h pattern, not a one-time miss.

Hypothesis A — Code regression: decider-intake.md Step 1d was wired up by task #91 in early May 2026. Something since then may have refactored the intake flow and broken the integrity-report-discovery path. Git log of decider-intake.md since 2026-05-05 would reveal it.

Hypothesis B — Model-class compatibility: decider's 01:30Z run was on Sonnet (header says 'Model: claude-sonnet-4-6'), not Opus. The intake path was authored when decider was Opus. Sonnet may be more literal about path conventions / file scanning patterns and could be silently skipping the integrity report if the intake step uses ambiguous reference (e.g. 'check integrity report' vs 'read monitor/integrity/latest-integrity-summary.txt and parse for tracked_under:null findings').

Hypothesis C — Step 1d filter rules: maybe Step 1d only handles 'moderate' severity findings (the original PROP-022 scope was 'moderate-findings handoff gap'). If so, CRITICAL + MAJOR findings would slip through. Check whether Step 1d's filter is too narrow.

Same shape as DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 (analyst summary-write gap): a broken handoff hides agent state from operator visibility. Pattern recognition matters — this is the second 'invisible-to-operator' failure in 48h. Worth understanding what design-pattern lets handoffs silently break.
operator_intent: Tinker investigates which of the three hypotheses (A code regression / B model-class / C filter-too-narrow) is the actual cause, confirms via git log + prompt-file read + decider report archaeology, proposes the canonical fix as PROP-NNN. The fix should ensure decider creates ISSs for EVERY integrity finding flagged 'tracked_under: null' regardless of severity, and should be model-class-agnostic (works on Sonnet AND Opus).

tasks.description: Read decider-intake.md Step 1d in full. Identify: (a) what file path it reads for integrity, (b) what severity filter it applies (if any), (c) what the trigger condition is for ISS creation, (d) whether the read is mode-agnostic or runs only in specific decider modes.
tasks.deliverable: A short audit: 'Step 1d reads X file, filters by Y, creates ISS when Z, runs in modes M.'

tasks.description: git log monitor/prompts/reference/decider-intake.md since 2026-05-05 (when task #91 landed). Identify whether the integrity-intake section was modified, moved, or refactored. Look specifically for changes that might have narrowed scope, changed path, or moved logic to a different file.
tasks.deliverable: Commit list with brief description of each. Conclude 'no relevant changes' OR 'commit X on date Y introduced the regression'.

tasks.description: Cross-reference: did decider's 01:30Z run process integrity at all? Check whether decider's daily-report has any integrity-related fields (integrity_findings_processed, integrity_report_mtime, etc). If yes, what did decider see? If no, the intake path silently failed. This narrows hypothesis A vs B.
tasks.deliverable: One-line conclusion: decider attempted integrity intake (failed because X) OR decider never attempted integrity intake (intake path entirely skipped).

tasks.description: Look at last 5 decider runs (the ones since integrity has been emitting tracked_under:null findings). Count ISSs created from integrity findings per run. If the count is 0 across all 5, this is a 5-run-deep pattern, not a one-off miss.
tasks.deliverable: Per-run count table. Identify the inflection point — was there a recent decider run that DID process integrity findings, indicating when the regression landed?

tasks.description: If hypothesis B (model-class) is plausible: identify the specific phrasing in Step 1d that could be Sonnet-unfriendly. Sonnet performs better with explicit file paths and rigid 'IF X THEN Y' conditionals; worse with judgment-cued 'consider the integrity findings and decide'. Compare the current Step 1d phrasing against this rubric.
tasks.deliverable: Verbatim quote of Step 1d's intake instruction + Sonnet-readability assessment.
tasks.conditional_on: Only execute if hypothesis B isn't ruled out by tasks 2-4.

tasks.description: Propose the canonical fix as PROP-NNN. Options to evaluate:

options.option_A_path_pin: Pin the integrity-intake path explicitly in Step 1d: read FUSE-canonical path monitor/integrity/latest-integrity-summary.txt, parse text for 'tracked_under: null' lines, extract severity/category/recommended_action, create ISS. Model-class-agnostic: no judgment required, just text extraction.
options.option_B_explicit_severity_loop: Expand Step 1d to explicitly iterate through findings by severity (CRITICAL → MAJOR → MODERATE → MINOR) and create one ISS per finding regardless of severity. Removes any implicit severity filter.
options.option_C_integrity_writes_machine_readab: Modify integrity script to emit a separate findings.json that decider reads via JSON.parse (not text grep). More robust but bigger change touching integrity prompt too.
options.option_D_hybrid: Option A + B: pin path AND explicit severity loop. Belt-and-suspenders.

design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Recommend one option with rationale. Operator preference unstated; default to the option that's most model-class-resilient.

Q2: Does the fix need to handle the case where decider runs more frequently than integrity (currently integrity daily, decider 2×/day)? Should decider re-process the same integrity report if it already created ISSs for it last run, or track 'last-processed integrity-report-mtime'?
Q3: Should integrity's findings have an 'integrity_finding_id' so decider can dedupe? Right now findings are positional ('first MAJOR', 'second MAJOR') — fragile for cross-run matching.

Q4: How does this interact with the analyst summary-write gap directive (DIRECTIVE-20260514-001)? Both are 'invisible handoff' failures. Worth a single meta-PROP about handoff robustness, or two separate PROPs?

tasks.description: Steelman the case for NOT fixing this aggressively. Strongest counter-arguments to address:
counter_args_to_address: 'Integrity findings should be operator-attention, not auto-ISS. The whole point of integrity is to surface things to a human; auto-creating ISSs sends them into the backlog where they get prioritized along with everything else and lose their dashboard-prominence.' Rebuttal expected: integrity findings DO need ISS-routing if they're going to be tracked and closed; the dashboard prominence can come from severity tier + age, not from skipping the ISS step.

'Operator handled all 5 findings in 45 minutes this morning — the failure isn't catastrophic. Maybe the right pattern is operator-applies-integrity in daily standups, not auto-route.' Rebuttal expected: this works for daily integrity but the URGENT tracker-allocator finding had a NEXT-RUN collision risk (every 2h baby run). 24h operator turnaround is too slow for URGENT.

'Sonnet decider may simply not be ready for intake work this complex. The fix isn't decider-intake.md; it's reverting decider to Opus.' Rebuttal expected: even on Opus, the previous 24h-old findings went un-ISSed. So model-class isn't the only factor.

tasks.description: Author the PROP-NNN as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, then operator + tinker apply prompt edits.

in_scope_for_tinker: Read decider-intake.md (especially Step 1d, which task #91 wired up)
Read last 5 decider daily-report-*.json files for evidence of integrity intake
Read latest integrity report and confirm what tracked_under:null findings look like in the source
Read CLAUDE.md File Ownership rules to confirm integrity report path
Author one PROP-NNN with the full design
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify decider-intake.md or any prompt directly. Propose-only.
Do NOT modify the integrity script. (Option C is a design proposal, not an implementation.)

Do NOT batch with DIRECTIVE-20260514-001 (analyst summary-write gap) or DIRECTIVE-20260514-002 (curmudgeon split). Each gets its own PROP. Q4 in design_questions_for_tinker addresses whether to harmonize.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: Counter-argument section mandatory (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too')
Recommend the most model-class-resilient option — Sonnet decider should work as well as Opus for this intake path

Identify URGENT-finding fast-path: the next_id=384 collision finding had a same-day collision risk; the design should ensure CRITICAL/MAJOR findings get ISSs created in the same decider run as integrity emits them, not the next run.
